title: Unicode Font Generator
description: Convert text into fancy Unicode styles — bold, italic, script, fraktur, double-struck, monospace, and more.
note: Latin letters (A–Z) and digits (0–9) are converted. Other characters are passed through unchanged.
empty: Type something above to see all font styles.

howToUse.label: Enter your text
howToUse.description: Type or paste text into the input field. Latin letters and digits are converted in real time.

howToUse.label: Browse the styles
howToUse.description: Scroll through 12 different Unicode font styles displayed instantly below the input.

howToUse.label: Copy and paste
howToUse.description: Click "Copy" on any style you like, then paste it into social media bios, posts, documents, or chats.

faq.question: Why do these font styles work everywhere?
faq.answer: They are not actual fonts — they use special Unicode characters that visually resemble styled letters (e.g., Mathematical Bold, Script, Double-Struck). Because they are real characters in the text, any app or website renders them the same way without needing font files.

faq.question: Can I use these on Instagram, X (Twitter), or Discord?
faq.answer: Yes. Unicode characters work in social media bios, usernames, posts, and chat messages. They are a popular way to add visual flair without relying on platform-specific formatting.

faq.question: Why aren't Japanese or emoji characters converted?
faq.answer: The Unicode mathematical letter blocks only define styled versions of Latin A–Z and digits 0–9. Characters outside that range — including CJK, emoji, and symbols — are passed through as-is.

faq.question: Are there any accessibility concerns?
faq.answer: Yes. Screen readers may pronounce these as 'mathematical bold capital A' rather than just 'A'. Avoid using them in important documents, headings, or any content that needs to be accessible.
